ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer (Male/Female) */,
  "senior_citizen" INTEGER /* Whether the customer is a senior citizen (0/1) */,
  "partner" TEXT /* Whether the customer has a partner (Yes/No) */,
  "dependents" TEXT /* Whether the customer has dependents (Yes/No) */,
  "tenure" INTEGER /* Tenure of the customer in months */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Primary contact phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the customer */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the customer's account (e.g., active, suspended, closed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the customer's account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the customer's account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service (e.g., Phone, Internet, TV) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Type of service (e.g., phone, internet, TV) */,
  "service_speed" REAL /* Speed of the service (for internet services) */,
  "service_bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth of the service (for internet and TV services) */,
  "service_features" TEXT /* List of features included in the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "plans" (
  "plan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each plan */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the plan belongs to */,
  "plan_name" TEXT /* Name of the plan */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the plan */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the plan */,
  "plan_speed" REAL /* Speed of the plan (for internet plans) */,
  "plan_bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth of the plan (for internet and TV plans) */,
  "plan_features" TEXT /* List of features included in the plan */,
  "plan_validity" TEXT /* Validity period of the plan (e.g., monthly, yearly) */,
  "plan_discount" REAL /* Discount applicable to the plan */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plan_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_services" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service subscribed */,
  "plan_id" INTEGER /* ID of the plan subscribed */,
  "subscription_date" TEXT /* Date the service was subscribed */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the service will end */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the service subscription (e.g., active, paused, terminated) */,
  "renewal_date" TEXT /* Date when the service will be renewed */,
  "auto_renew" TEXT /* Whether the service should automatically renew (Yes/No) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the service subscription was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id", "service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_services_plan_id FOREIGN KEY ("plan_id") REFERENCES plans ("plan_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payments" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., credit card, bank transfer) */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date the payment was made */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of the payment */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "transaction_id" TEXT /* Transaction ID for reference */,
  "payment_gateway" TEXT /* Payment gateway used (e.g., PayPal, Stripe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payments_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "billing" (
  "bill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bill */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "bill_date" TEXT /* Date the bill was generated */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Date the bill is due */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount of the bill */,
  "bill_status" TEXT /* Status of the bill (e.g., pending, paid, overdue) */,
  "payment_due" REAL /* Amount still due on the bill */,
  "late_fee" REAL /* Late fees applied to the bill */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bill_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_billing_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "churn" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "churn_date" TEXT /* Date the customer churned */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for churning */,
  "churn_type" TEXT /* Type of churn (e.g., voluntary, involuntary) */,
  "follow_up" TEXT /* Follow-up actions taken after the customer churned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date the feedback was given */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "feedback_type" TEXT /* Type of feedback (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Numerical rating (e.g., 1-5 stars) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "support_tickets" (
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ticket */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "ticket_date" TEXT /* Date the ticket was raised */,
  "issue" TEXT /* Issue reported by the customer */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution provided by the support team */,
  "ticket_status" TEXT /* Status of the ticket (e.g., open, in progress, resolved) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the ticket (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the support agent handling the ticket */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time taken to resolve the ticket */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_support_tickets_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., phone, laptop) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "device_status" TEXT /* Status of the device (e.g., active, inactive, lost) */,
  "last_used" TEXT /* Date and time when the device was last used */,
  "device_serial" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "usage_date" TEXT /* Date the data was used */,
  "data_used" REAL /* Amount of data used in GB */,
  "usage_type" TEXT /* Type of data usage (e.g., mobile, fixed) */,
  "usage_location" TEXT /* Location where the data was used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_usage_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "calls" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each call */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "call_date" TEXT /* Date the call was made */,
  "call_duration" REAL /* Duration of the call in minutes */,
  "call_type" TEXT /* Type of call (e.g., local, international) */,
  "call_direction" TEXT /* Direction of the call (e.g., incoming, outgoing) */,
  "call_location" TEXT /* Location where the call was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_calls_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sms" (
  "sms_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each SMS record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "sms_date" TEXT /* Date the SMS was sent */,
  "sms_count" INTEGER /* Number of SMS sent */,
  "sms_type" TEXT /* Type of SMS (e.g., text, multimedia) */,
  "sms_direction" TEXT /* Direction of the SMS (e.g., incoming, outgoing) */,
  "sms_location" TEXT /* Location where the SMS was sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sms_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sms_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
